title: End-to-End RAG Architecture, Versioning, Caches, and Failures · Program Brief: Rag Cache Key Invalidation
summary: A prototype hides ingestion, retrieval, reranking, generation, and citation inside one framework call, caches by query text alone, and cannot reproduce which corpus or prompt produced an answer. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cache key invalidation increment in versioned end-to-end RAG service. Learners…
objectives: Design embedding, retrieval, reranking, context, and answer cache keys and invalidation from every behavior-changing input and authorization boundary.
Bind each run to corpus, document, chunk, parser, embedding, index, query, reranker, prompt, generator, policy, and evaluation versions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-pipeline-architecture-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-pipeline-architecture-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_pipeline_architecture_4(corpus_version: int = 12, embedding_version: int = 7, index_version: int = 12, prompt_version: int = 9, stage_count: int = 6, idempotent: bool = True, cache_key_complete: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if min(corpus_version, embedding_version, index_version, prompt_version) < 1:
        return False, 0, "version-contract"
    if index_version != corpus_version:
        return False, stage_count, "index-corpus-skew"
    if stage_count < 5:
        return False, stage_count, "missing-stage"
    if not idempotent or not cache_key_complete:
        return False, stage_count, "replay-cache"
    identity = corpus_version + embedding_version + prompt_version
    assert identity > index_version
    return True, identity, "versioned-pipeline"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Cache Key Invalidation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 8018 during a multimodal maintenance-manual release. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose versioned ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, and cache contracts; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design embedding, retrieval, reranking, context, and answer cache keys and invalidation from every behavior-changing input and authorization boundary.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Cache Key Invalidation model · Theory Rag Cache Key Invalidation.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Cache Key Invalidation model · Theory Rag Cache Key Invalidation, keep Rag Cache Key Invalidation connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design embedding, retrieval, reranking, context, and answer cache keys and invalidation from every behavior-changing input and authorization boundary.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Cache Key Invalidation model · Theory Rag Cache Key Invalidation, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Cache Key Invalidation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Cache Key Invalidation model · Theory Rag Cache Key Invalidation, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 8018 during a multimodal maintenance-manual release. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose versioned ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, and cache contracts; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design embedding, retrieval, reranking, context, and answer cache keys and invalidation from every behavior-changing input and authorization boundary.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Cache Key Invalidation model · Theory Rag Cache Key Invalidation, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: Query text alone is a safe RAG response-cache key.
